name: Natural Monument Nacimiento del Río Cuervo from Vega del Codorno
name_original: Monumento Natural Nacimiento del Río Cuervo desde Vega del Codorno
region_intro: Hiking around Vega Del Codorno offers access to the Serranía de Cuenca mountain range, characterized by its mountainous terrain and extensive pine forests. The region features significant river systems, including the Júcar River and the Cuervo River, known for its source with waterfalls and travertine springs. These natural elements create a diverse landscape for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Vega Del Codorno The most popular hiking route is Dehesa de los Olmos Trail, a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 31 minutes to complete, leading through diverse forests within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cerro San Felipe - circular through the Alto Tajo Natural Park (PR-CU 79), a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path offering views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Local hikers also love the Natural Monument Nacimiento del Río Cuervo from Vega del Codorno, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through river valleys to the impressive source of the Cuervo River, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Vega Del Codorno is defined by mountainous terrain, dense pine forests, and significant river sources. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to longer routes exploring the natural monuments. The routes in Vega Del Codorno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Vega Del Codorno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What natural monuments or impressive waterfalls can I explore in Vega Del Codorno?
A: The most prominent natural monument is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt;, famous for its breathtaking waterfalls and "chorreras" over moss-covered limestone. You can reach it via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013222502/natural-monument-nacimiento-del-rio-cuervo-from-vega-del-codorno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Monument Nacimiento del Río Cuervo from Vega del Codorno&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuervo River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are also notable features in the area.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Vega Del Codorno?
A: Yes, the path to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt; is generally easy, circular, and suitable for all ages, making it ideal for family outings. The region offers various paths through forests and mountains that cater to different fitness levels, including easier options.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Vega Del Codorno?
A: Vega Del Codorno is nestled within the Serranía de Cuenca mountain range, so you can expect mountainous terrain covered with extensive pine forests. The region is also characterized by significant river systems like the Júcar and Cuervo, contributing to diverse landscapes with clean water and fresh air, and unique geological features like travertine springs.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013326853/cerro-san-felipe-circular-through-the-alto-tajo-natural-park-pr-cu-79" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro San Felipe - circular through the Alto Tajo Natural Park (PR-CU 79)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013227142/cueva-del-nacimiento-de-vega-del-codorno-circular-to-muela-de-san-felipe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Nacimiento de Vega del Codorno – circular to Muela de San Felipe&lt;/a&gt;, providing views from the Mirador de la Muela de San Felipe.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vega Del Codorno?
A: Vega Del Codorno experiences a mountain climate. Summers are generally mild and pleasant, making them ideal for hiking. While winters can bring snow, spring and autumn offer beautiful scenery with comfortable temperatures for exploration.

Q: What are the options for parking when accessing hiking trails?
A: While specific parking locations for every trailhead are not detailed, the region is generally accessible for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage or information centers upon arrival for designated parking areas, especially near popular spots like the Nacimiento del Río Cuervo.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while hiking in Vega Del Codorno?
A: The extensive pine forests and preserved old-growth areas are home to diverse wildlife. You might spot deer, wild boars, and various bird species. The nearby Parque Cinegético El Hosquillo also offers a unique opportunity to observe bears, wolves, deer, and ibex in a semi-wild environment.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, among the 17 routes available, 5 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous walk. The path to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt; is also known for its accessibility.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Vega Del Codorno?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the impressive river sources, particularly the Nacimiento del Río Cuervo.

Q: Are there any regulations or permits required for hiking in Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park?
A: The Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park is a protected area. While general hiking on marked trails typically doesn't require permits, it's always good practice to respect park regulations, stay on designated paths, and be mindful of the natural environment. Specific activities or access to certain restricted areas might have different rules.

Q: What is the longest moderate hike available in Vega Del Codorno?
A: The longest moderate hike is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013397511/dehesa-de-los-olmos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de los Olmos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 16.0 km (9.9 miles) and leads through diverse forests within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.

Q: Can I explore any caves in the vicinity of Vega Del Codorno?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013227142/cueva-del-nacimiento-de-vega-del-codorno-circular-to-muela-de-san-felipe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Nacimiento&lt;/a&gt;, located near the Cuervo River's source, offers an interesting exploration opportunity. Its initial 50 meters can be traversed without special caving equipment.
tips:
The path to the source of the Cuervo River is a wonderful experience and accessible to everyone.
The source of the Cuervo River is located in the Serranía de Cuenca, near the town of Vega del Codorno. From Cuenca, you can take the CM-2105 road and then the CM-2106 until you reach the source parking lot. 🌄🚶‍♀️😎
The waterfalls are spectacular waterfalls that run down rocks covered with vegetation.😎🌄🚶‍♀️

The Birth of the Cuervo River is a dream place in the Serranía de Cuenca, Spain. This Natural Monument houses a main waterfall with a height of more than 20 meters, leaving those who contemplate it breathless. Here, the Cuervo River springs from a spring of travertine origin, flowing over limestone rock covered with moss, forming beautiful waterfalls called chorreras.

waypoints.name: Path to the source of the Cuervo River
